name: Pesto Potato Salad
recipeIngredient: 16 large Fresh basil leaves, spun dry
1/2 cup Fresh parsley, packed and spun dry
2 tbsp Pine nuts, toasted until golden
recipeInstructions:
1. In a kettle 2/3 full of boiling salted water, cool potatoes with caraway seeds for 45 minutes, or until tender.
2. Make pesto while the potatoes are boiling. Mix all pesto ingredients (except olive oil) in a food processor until basil and parsley are chopped fine, scraping down sides. With the motor running, add oil in a slow stream and process until pesto is smooth.
3. Drain potatoes, discarding caraway seeds; cool.
4. When potatoes are cool enough to handle, peel and cut into 1/4-inch slices, transferring to a large heatproof bowl.
5. In a small non-reactive saucepan, bring the remaining ingredients (red wine vinegar, beef broth, Dijon mustard, salt, pepper) to a boil; pour over potatoes.
6. Toss potatoes with the mixture and let stand 15 minutes.
7. Drain potatoes in a colander, discarding liquid, and return to bowl.
8. Add pesto, tossing gently to coat.
